ReleaseLabel: Extension 0.32.0 (Set 063 'Getting Started budget step + adoption-bootstrap retirement'); Marketplace-only, no PyPI bump
Notes: Ad-hoc per-set UAT gating the 0.32.0 Marketplace publish. UAT runs against a LOCAL build (.vsix from `npm run package`, or the F5 dev host) — never a published one (Set 058 lesson: the mechanical suites run on a vscode-stub and never exercise real activation/commands/webviews). DESIGNED FOR A SHORT OPERATOR PASS (~10 minutes): every mechanically-coverable row was pre-verified by the AI orchestrator on 2026-06-12 (Passes=true, the deterministic check named in ProgrammaticVerification) against this suite baseline: Python 1222 passed + 1 skipped; TS unit 908 passing with exactly the 2 tracked pre-existing Set-026 failures (configEditor-foundation, notificationsSection) — the agreed-green baseline; Playwright Layer 3 LOCAL 18 passed (4.7m); drift guard OK. The operator walk is the 'Operator walk' items only, in order. Walks 2-6 need a FRESH EMPTY FOLDER (any throwaway directory) opened in the dev-host/extension window so the Getting Started form renders; walk 8 needs a folder WITH session sets (this repo works).

Review.Subarea: Operator walk 1 — local build installs and activates
Review.ItemLabel: The local 0.32.0-candidate .vsix installs and the extension activates cleanly
Review.HumanAction: Install the freshly built candidate .vsix (`code --install-extension tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/dabbler-ai-orchestration-0.31.0.vsix` — built 2026-06-12 from the release-candidate tree; the version metadata bumps to 0.32.0 only after this UAT passes, same bits otherwise) or launch the F5 dev host. Open the Dabbler activity-bar icon.
Review.Expectation: Extension activates without error; the Session Set Explorer view renders (form or tree depending on the folder).
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: activationNoFolder.test.ts pins activation order on the stub; real activation is exactly what the stub cannot exercise (the Set 058 lesson) — this is the live portion.

Review.Subarea: Operator walk 2 — Full tier shows the budget input
Review.ItemLabel: Fresh empty folder + Full tier: a required budget/NTE input renders inside the Build-project-structure step
Review.HumanAction: Open a fresh empty folder. In the Getting Started form's step 1, select the Full tier radio. Look for the budget input (placeholder example: 25) above/beside the Build button.
Review.Expectation: The budget input is present and marked required on Full tier; copy explains it is the verification budget / NTE cap written to ai_router/budget.yaml.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: gettingStartedHtml.test.ts — 'Full tier renders the budget input inside step 1, before the Build button'; TS suite green at the tracked baseline 2026-06-12.
Review.Feedback: I am wondering if we should have a dropdown or radio for what the budget constrains: e.g., (a) total project spend, (b) daily spend ... etc.

Review.ItemLabel: Empty / non-numeric / negative budget input blocks the Build action with inline validation
Review.HumanAction: Still on Full tier: try to Build with the budget input empty, then with 'abc', then with -5.
Review.Expectation: Each attempt is blocked with an inline validation message; no scaffold happens and no budget.yaml appears.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: gettingStartedHtml.test.ts — 'validateBudgetControls: invalid input blocks with the parse message'; host boundary is independently FAIL-CLOSED (gettingStartedActions.test.ts — 'REJECTS a Full build-structure whose budget rider does not narrow (R1 fail-closed fix)').

Review.Subarea: Operator walk 4 — $0 requires an explicit verification rule
Review.ItemLabel: Entering 0 reveals the required radio pair (manual-via-other-engine / skipped) with the locked copy; Build stays blocked until one is picked
Review.HumanAction: Enter 0 in the budget input. Read the revealed copy. Try Build without picking a radio; then pick one.
Review.Expectation: Copy reads: 'A $0 budget still needs a verification rule. Choose whether to check each session in another engine or skip verification.' — Build is blocked until 'Check in another engine' or 'Skip verification' is selected. No silent default.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: gettingStartedHtml.test.ts — '$0 input reveals the zero-rule radio pair with the locked copy' + 'validateBudgetControls: $0 blocks until a zero-rule is picked'.

Review.Subarea: Operator walk 5 — Build writes budget.yaml in the audited shape
Review.ItemLabel: A valid amount (e.g. 25) + Build scaffolds the project and lands ai_router/budget.yaml in the post-migration contract shape
Review.HumanAction: Enter 25, click Build project structure, let the scaffold finish, then open ai_router/budget.yaml in the new folder.
Review.Expectation: File exists with: threshold_usd: 25, scope: "per-project", mode: "middle-tier", verification_method: "api", verification_nte_usd: 25, set_at (ISO local w/ offset), set_by: "getting-started-form", warn_at_percent: 80. No threshold_scope, no notes.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: budgetYaml.test.ts — 'renderBudgetYaml — the §2.4 post-migration contract shape' suite (all four mode bands, editor-schema acceptance via validateBatch, migrator-no-op TS twin) + 'scaffoldConsumerRepo — budget write (Set 063 S2, spec D1)'.

Review.Subarea: Operator walk 6 — Lightweight omits the step
Review.ItemLabel: Switching the tier radio to Lightweight removes the budget block from the form entirely; Build proceeds without it and writes no budget.yaml
Review.HumanAction: In a fresh empty folder (or the same form before building), select the Lightweight tier radio. Inspect step 1; optionally Build and check the scaffold output.
Review.Expectation: No budget input anywhere in the form (omitted from the DOM, not just hidden — R1 Minor fix); the scaffold completes without ai_router/budget.yaml (and without router-config.yaml, the standing Set 058 D3 divergence).
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: gettingStartedHtml.test.ts — 'Lightweight OMITS the budget block entirely (never renders the input)'; budgetYaml.test.ts — 'Lightweight NEVER writes budget.yaml, even when a budget rider arrives'.

Review.Subarea: Operator walk 7 — command gone from the palette
Review.ItemLabel: 'Dabbler: Copy adoption bootstrap prompt' no longer exists in the command palette
Review.HumanAction: Ctrl+Shift+P, type 'adoption' and then 'bootstrap'. Also type 'Dabbler:' and scan the command list.
Review.Expectation: No adoption-bootstrap command appears; the remaining Dabbler commands all resolve (no 'command not found' entries).
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: package.json contribution + registration + command file removed in S2 (commits a0b82fd); repo grep shows no live copyAdoptionBootstrapPrompt reference. Palette visibility on a real build is the live portion.

Review.ItemLabel: A folder WITH session sets renders the normal Explorer tree; an empty folder renders the Getting Started form; no welcome-HTML flash or blank view in either
Review.HumanAction: Open this repo (or any project with session sets) in the dev-host/extension window and watch the Session Set Explorer render. Then re-open the empty folder from walk 2.
Review.Expectation: Sets folder: loading sentinel → bucketed tree (no flash of welcome content, no blank panel). Empty folder: loading sentinel → Getting Started form.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: Playwright Layer 3 LOCAL 18 passed 2026-06-12 (loading-state.spec.ts 'Getting Started form renders after scan completes' + session-sets-tree.spec.ts sentinel-replacement + tree-structure specs); SnapshotPayload.gettingStarted is now REQUIRED (type-level resurrection-path closure).

Review.ItemLabel: The budget step's copy and layout read clearly (placement beside the key warning, $0 copy is understandable to a first-time user)
Review.HumanAction: Glance over the budget step as walked in items 2-5.
Review.Expectation: The step reads as an integral part of Build-project-structure; the $0 radio copy makes the choice's consequence clear.

Review.ItemLabel: Writer unit matrix: four mode bands, $0 with each method, narrowing, no-clobber, editor-schema acceptance, migrator no-op
Review.Expectation: deriveBudgetMode bands (0/zero, <20/limited, 20-99/middle, 100+/ample); $0 + manual-via-other-engine and $0 + skipped both render; untrusted riders narrow or reject; an existing budget.yaml is never clobbered (skip + report); rendered YAML passes the config editor's BUDGET_SCHEMA and is a no-op under _migrate_budget (TS twin).
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: budgetYaml.test.ts full file (suites: deriveBudgetMode, asBudgetUsd/asZeroBudgetMethod, resolveVerificationMethod, renderBudgetYaml, writeBudgetYaml incl. 'never clobbers an existing budget.yaml (skip + report)', scaffoldConsumerRepo budget write) — TS 908 passing at the tracked baseline 2026-06-12.

Review.ItemLabel: Full build-structure requests with an un-narrowable budget rider are REJECTED at the host (webview validation alone is not enforcement)
Review.Expectation: routeGettingStartedAction rejects rather than scaffolding budgetless on Full; palette setupNewProject remains the only budgetless entry.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: gettingStartedActions.test.ts — 'REJECTS a Full build-structure whose budget rider does not narrow (R1 fail-closed fix)' + asBudgetChoice narrowing suite; pinned since the S2 verification round 1 Major.

Review.ItemLabel: All 11 inventoried surfaces removed; gettingStarted REQUIRED on SnapshotPayload; scanState context key gone (ScanState manager + webview scanState messages stay)
Review.Expectation: Command file, registration, contribution, viewsWelcome, welcome-HTML pipe (host loader/renderer, protocol field, client.js fallback, .welcome CSS), escHtml/escAttr, scanState context key, and the Marketplace description phrase are gone per the S1 §1.1 inventory; compile is green with gettingStarted required.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: S2 shipped + verified (s2-verification-round-2.md VERIFIED); watcherInventory.test.ts allowlist updated same-commit (192/228 → 191/227); TS suite + Playwright green at baseline 2026-06-12.

Review.ItemLabel: adoption-bootstrap.md is a URL-stable redirect stub; budget-yaml-schema.md is the canonical contract home; no live doc still describes the retired path/button
Review.HumanAction: None required — pre-verified. (Optional 60-second spot-check: open docs/adoption-bootstrap.md and docs/budget-yaml-schema.md.)
Review.Expectation: Stub keeps the path alive for ≤0.31.0 raw-URL fetchers and redirects VS Code users to the form and manual users to quick-start; the schema doc publishes the post-migration shape with the legacy-compat table (threshold_scope→scope, absent verification_method→api, absent scope→per-project marked DERIVED); quick-start/READMEs/workflow doc/repository-reference/tier-model all swept.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: drift_guard.py OK post-sweep 2026-06-12; repo-wide grep shows only deliberate references (stub, schema doc, historical narratives, CHANGELOGs/session-set artifacts which are never edited); D3 executed per the S1 §4 lock; covered by this session's cross-provider verification round.

Review.ItemLabel: Python, TS unit, and Playwright Layer 3 all green at the agreed baseline on the release candidate
Review.Expectation: Python 1222 passed + 1 skipped; TS unit 908 passing + exactly the 2 tracked pre-existing Set-026 failures; Playwright LOCAL 18 passed; drift guard OK.
Review.ProgrammaticVerification: Live runs 2026-06-12 (Set 063 S3): pytest 1222/1 skipped in 3m17s; mocha 908 passing/2 failing (Set-026 baseline); npx playwright test 18 passed in 4.7m; ai_router/scripts/drift_guard.py OK.
